full_response:
Let

v(λ) = (x(λ), y(λ), z(λ))  
    = (sin(λ²), ln(λ+1), 2λ² − 1).

The tangent vector to the curve is the derivative dv/dλ:

1. x-component  
 x(λ) = sin(λ²)  
 dx/dλ = cos(λ²) · d(λ²)/dλ = cos(λ²) · 2λ = 2λ cos(λ²).

2. y-component  
 y(λ) = ln(λ+1)  (valid for λ > −1)  
 dy/dλ = 1/(λ+1).

3. z-component  
 z(λ) = 2λ² − 1  
 dz/dλ = 4λ.

Therefore

dv/dλ = (2λ cos(λ²), 1/(λ+1), 4λ).

\boxed{\displaystyle \frac{dv}{d\lambda} = \bigl(2\lambda\cos(\lambda^{2}),\; \frac{1}{\lambda+1},\; 4\lambda\bigr)}

Confidence: 91%
